Program Learning Objectives

MENU

Program Learning Objectives

Graduates of the Economics and Finance Program

Scientific and Analytical Competency

Work as analysts or researchers in the fields of economics and finance, combining theory and models with applied analysis to provide scientific solutions to economic and financial problems.

Data Analysis and Technology Competency

Play an active role in data analysis and decision making in business and academic environments; collect and analyse quantitative and qualitative data, and effectively use information technologies in these processes.

Global Perspective and Interdisciplinary Communication Competency

Monitor global developments in economics and finance, communicate effectively across disciplines, and participate in international and/or multidisciplinary projects.

Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Leadership Skills

Develop innovative ideas, evaluate entrepreneurial opportunities, and assume leadership roles in projects or team-based work.

Ethical Values and Social Responsibility Awareness

Act in accordance with professional and social ethical principles in private, public, or academic settings, and take responsibility, make decisions, and take action in line with sustainability and societal benefit.

Lifelong Learning and Self-Development Competency

Adapt to evolving knowledge and technologies in economics and finance, and pursue continuous learning and professional development.
